title: Navitas Stock Jumps After $122 Million Raise: Why The AI Power Chip Bet Matters Now
text: Navitas Semiconductor completed a significant equity offering, securing approximately $122 million in net proceeds through an at-the-market share sale program initiated two days prior. By May 12, the company had sold 6,529,666 Class A shares, with additional trades pending settlement. The semiconductor firm's shares jumped 10.5% to $21.28 during U.S. trading, elevating its market capitalization to nearly $4.9 billion. Trading ranged between $19.68 and $21.98, with over 26 million shares exchanging hands. Management positions this capital raise as backing a strategic pivot away from legacy mobile charger businesses toward high-power chips serving AI data centers, power grids, and industrial applications. CEO Chris Allexandre emphasized India as a key market for this repositioning effort. On May 11, Cyient Semiconductors announced launching seven gallium nitride (GaN) power devices utilizing Navitas technology for the Indian market. GaN technology enables more efficient power handling with reduced heat generation compared to traditional silicon solutions. Despite investor enthusiasm, Navitas faces headwinds: first-quarter revenue declined to $8.6 million from $14.0 million year-over-year, while GAAP operating losses reached $27.8 million. The company competes against larger rivals including Infineon Technologies and Texas Instruments.
